summary: Tests thoroughly cover core labeler.ts functions (glob matching, rule parsing, label assignment) and workflow structure validation. However, ~35-40% of the codebase—the entire cli.ts module (parseArgs, readFileList, main, help output)—is untested at the unit level, relying only on indirect integration testing through act. Edge case coverage is sparse: missing tests for empty patterns, malformed CLI inputs, file I/O errors, Unicode/special characters, and same label with conflicting priorities. The workflow integration tests are well-structured but depend on undocumented fixture directories and use fragile regex-based output parsing for label extraction. While the suite would catch regressions in core labeling logic, the CLI gaps and limited error-scenario testing mean meaningful bugs could ship.
